journal_entry.reason: Land is recorded at the present value of the deferred payment obligation. Using the present value of an ordinary annuity formula: PV = $10,000,000 × 4.917324 (PV factor for 6 periods at 6%) = $49,173,240. The existing building to be demolished is included in the land cost as its value cannot be separated and demolition is necessary to prepare the land for its intended use. The liability represents the present value of 6 annual payments of $10,000,000 at 6% interest.

output_debit_classifier.reason: This transaction involves the acquisition of land with an existing building for $60,000,000, to be paid in 6 equal annual installments of $10,000,000 starting at the end of the year of acquisition. Since payment is deferred, the land should be recorded at the present value of the future payments using the 6% market interest rate. The present value of an ordinary annuity of $10,000,000 for 6 periods at 6% is approximately $49,173,240 (using PV factor of 4.917324). The existing building will be demolished immediately, so its value is considered part of the land cost (demolition costs are capitalized to land). The journal entry records: (1) Debit to Land for the present value of $49,173,240, and (2) Credit to Long-term Notes/Loan Payable for $49,173,240. The difference between the total payments ($60,000,000) and present value ($49,173,240) represents interest expense to be recognized over the 6-year period.
